note: C-37/C-26 attested provenance chain. Every SHIPPED production component (what GitHub Pages serves) is listed with its content hash and the chain that produced it: the frozen specification it derives from, the policy bundle that gated it, the test evidence, the adversarial-verification result, and the NAMED OWNING ROLE answerable for it. verify/tests/61-provenance verifies (a) every shipped file is listed, (b) each hash matches the file on disk (a silent change to a shipped component breaks the chain and fails the build), and (c) the chain fields are non-empty. This is git-anchored and machine-reconstructible on demand. RESIDUAL (R-LEDGER, honest): the model+prompt-hash per commit is NOT cryptographically signed here; git history + this manifest are the append-only substitute. Full Runner-only signed provenance is the buildable upgrade. | v2.1 (compiled-ahead): served set is index.html + compiled ./*.js + self-hosted ./vendor/* ; the .jsx are attested SOURCE, and 66-compiled-fresh proves compiled==source.
policy_bundle_version: verify/ @ catalogue v2.0 (run.js + mutation.js + gate.js + tests/)

reconstruction_control: verify/tests/61-provenance reconstructs the chain for every shipped component on every build and fails loudly if a component is unlisted or its hash drifted; the git history supplies which commit/model authored each change. A scheduled spot-reconstruction is the standing cadence (governance/mandate cadence table).
